message: 🌱 Add open-policy-agent-opa-: Allow parameterized tests mission (#577)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "open-policy-agent-opa-2176-allow-parameterized-tests",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "open-policy-agent-opa-: Allow parameterized tests",
+    "description": "Allow parameterized tests. Requested by 12+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current open-policy-agent-opa- deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your open-policy-agent-opa- version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-\nhelm list -n open-policy-agent-opa- 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working open-policy-agent-opa- installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review open-policy-agent-opa- configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant open-policy-agent-opa- configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-\nkubectl get configmap -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-policy-agent-opa-\n```\nIt would be very useful if the unit test system allowed for some type of simple \"data driven\" parameterization of tests where one of the mocked inputs could be parameterized to skip needless verbosity and possible copy-paste errors."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Allow parameterized tests",
+        "description": "Adding ability to declare multiple \"test cases\" within a Rego `test_` rule, which is then individually reported (grouped under their parent rule) when running `opa test`. E.g.:\n\n```rego\npackage example_test\n\ntest_concat[note] if {\n\tsome note, tc in {\n\t\t\"empty + empty\": {\n\t\t\t\"a\": [],\n\t\t\t\"b\":\n```yaml\ntest_method_not_allowed[input.method] {\n    not allow with input.method as [\"POST\", \"DELETE\", \"PUT\", \"PATCH\"]\n}\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-\nkubectl get events -n open-policy-agent-opa- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Allow parameterized tests\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Adding ability to declare multiple \"test cases\" within a Rego `test_` rule, which is then individually reported (grouped under their parent rule) when running `opa test`.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "test_method_not_allowed[input.method] {\n    not allow with input.method as [\"POST\", \"DELETE\", \"PUT\", \"PATCH\"]\n}",
+        "Running `opa test -v .` would give something like:",
+        "Another case, albeit quite a different one, I've been looking at recently is piping kubernetes resource lists into conftest and the like (`kubectl get deployments -o yaml | conftest test -`) with a corresponding policy like the below:"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "open-policy-agent-opa-",
+      "graduated",
+      "security",
+      "feature"
+    ],
